Posted on May 2

   

🎉We are excited to announce our refine's first open-source cloud product: refine.new

refine.new enables you to create React-based, headless UI enterprise applications within your browser that you can preview, tweak and download instantly.

By visually combining options for your preferred ➡️React platform, ➡️UI framework, ➡️backend connector, and ➡️auth. provider; you can create tailor-made architectures for your project in seconds. It feels like having access to thousands of project templates at your fingertips, allowing you to choose the one that best suits your needs! 🚀

Once you have made your selections, a live preview is immediately accessible, and you can customize it further by choosing themes and logos. 🎨

Here is an example of a complete CRUD app built using React and Material UI on refine.new.

refine.new/preview/7281d6a6-4929-4657-a…



🏄 If you're a busy developer, you can quickly download the final version of the application by clicking on the "Build & Download" button.

For those who are new to refine, it is an open-source, headless React framework for rapidly building enterprise web applications. It eliminates many repetitive tasks like

➡️ CRUD operations ➡️ State management ➡️ Routing ➡️ Authentication ➡️ Access Control

➡️ i18n

and saves days of development time.

🚀 refine GitHub Repository: github.com/refinedev/refine

Thanks to it's unique "Headless UI" approach, refine works with any custom design. It has also ready-made integrations with 4 most popular UI frameworks: Ant Design, Material UI, Chakra UI and Mantine. refine is totally backend agnostic and it can connect to 15+ backend services out-of-the-box, incl. custom REST & GraphQL API's.

With refine, developers always have 100% control over their projects as they build, style and deploy the way they like.

For further information, you may visit our website or GitHub repo. Our documentation includes hundreds of real-life examples and many tutorials to dive in.

refine tutorial: refine.dev/docs/tutorial/introduction/i…

If you have any questions, ideas or suggestion please write them in the comments below. The team will be here all day to answer. 🙋‍♂️

Again, happy coding to everyone! ❤️

Items.Title: 使用 NextJS 和 React 构建课程登陆页面 | Building a course landing page with NextJS and React
Items.Description:
过去一年我一直在Novu工作，获得了疯狂的成长
你能相信我们在短短一年内获得了20,000星星吗？ 这让我开始思考如何帮助其他人发展他们的 GitHub 库，所以我创建了GitHub 20k 课程。

但这很无聊。 重要的是我开源了它
我想告诉你我是怎么做到的:)

建筑学

首先，我必须决定技术。 我想让任何开发人员尽可能轻松地将它用于个人用途。

所以我决定选择 NextJS，因为我可以使用 NextJS 后端——而不是一个单独的项目或一个单一的 repo。

还有 Tailwind，这样就不需要 CSS 了。

没有数据库，一切都存储在 JSON 中，我创建了多个提供者，这样人们就可以为自己制作他们需要的东西或为项目做出贡献。

流量

我想以最简单的方式创建销售页面。

所以我们只有三个阶段。

  1. 你去销售页面
  2. 你去收银台
  3. 你触发多项服务

当我完成它时，我说，为什么不建一个博客呢？
所以我添加了一个基于无头 WordPress 的博客，但我把所有东西都打包了，这样你就可以使用任何其他服务，比如 Sanity。

当你去博客时，你会发现它不是设计师制作的🤗

我实际上使用 ChatGPT 绘制了一些网格，因为我对它们很不满意。

设计

通常最难找到的东西之一是设计师。 有很多，但他们的大部分作品通常看起来都一样。

我不得不多次扫描 Dribbble 才能找到我喜欢的东西。

有些人可能会说它可以更加图形化，而且人们不能专注于页面的内容，但我喜欢它。

如果您想使用登录页面但不确定如何使用，请阅读使用Docusaurus制作的文档

你能帮我吗？

我一直在非常努力地让每个需要登陆页面的人都可以使用它。 我非常喜欢这个社区❤️ 成为朋友，帮我打个星

一旦暂停，github20k 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

一旦解除暂停，github20k 将可以再次评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，github20k 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 github20k 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 github20k 的方法：

降低 github20k 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 github20k 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

I have been working for Novu over the last year and have gained crazy growth
Can you believe that we got 20,000 stars in just one year? 🤩

It got me thinking about how to help other people grow their GitHub libraries, so I created the GitHub 20k course.

But that's boring. The important part is that I open-sourced it
github.com/github-20k/super-star

And I want to tell you how I did it :)

Architecture

First, I had to decide about the tech. I wanted to make it as easy as possible for any developer to use it for their personal use.

So I decided to go for NextJS because I can use NextJS backend - instead of a separate project or a mono repo.

And also with Tailwind so that CSS won't be necessary.

There is no database, everything is stored in JSONs, and I created multiple providers so people could make what they need for themselves or contribute to the project.

The flow

I wanted to make the easiest possible way to create a sales page.

So all we have is three stages.

  1. You go to the sales page
  2. You go to a checkout
  3. you trigger multiple services

When I completed it, I said, why not have a blog?
So I added a blog based on headless WordPress, but I wrapped everything up so you can use any other service such as Sanity.

When you go to the blog, you can see it was not a designer that made it 🤣

I actually used ChatGPT to draw some grids because I am so bad with them.

Design

One of the hardest things to find usually is, designers. There are many, but most of their work usually looks the same.

I had to scan Dribbble many times to find something I liked.

Some might say it could be more graphical, and people can't focus on the page's content, but I like it.

If you want to use the landing page and are not sure how, just read the docs, made with Docusaurus

Can you help me out?

I have been working super hard to make it available for everyone needing a landing page. I love this community so much ❤️ Be a friend, and help me with a star

github.com/github-20k/super-star

Ezinne Anne😎👩🏿‍💻 - Apr 23

LABOUARDY Mohamed - Mar 31

Items.Title: 如何将应用内通知添加到 Angular 应用 | How To Add In-App Notifications To Your Angular App
Items.Description:
新皮尔斯曼

发表于 5 月 2 日

这篇文章解释了如何将应用内通知中心添加到您的 Angular 应用程序，并给了您一些微笑的理由 🙃

在其中一张截图中，提到了我最喜欢的一部电影。 如果您能正确猜出它是哪部电影并在评论部分留下您的答案，我一定会在我即将发表的文章中以某种方式向您致谢。 现在你很高兴，我们可以开始教程了。

可以使用许多对您的用例来说可能方便或必要的通知。

以下是您可能熟悉的一些通知（通信）渠道：

  • 应用内
  • 短信
  • 电子邮件
  • 聊天（Discord、Slack、MS Teams、WhatsApp、Telegram）
  • 推

然而，我们只会吃掉这个美味馅饼的 1/5 - 应用内通知。 此通信渠道允许您在应用程序中与用户进行交互。

假设你在过去的十年里没有生活在岩石之下，让我们观察一个基于应用程序内事件的通知工作流的例子，它适用于像 Instagram 这样的社交媒体应用程序：

  1. 用户打开 Instagram 应用程序并滚动浏览他们的动态，查看其他用户发布的照片​​和视频。
  2. 用户将照片或视频发布到他们自己的 Instagram 个人资料中。
  3. 另一个用户看到了帖子并喜欢它。
  4. 通知出现在用户的屏幕上，通知他们另一个用户喜欢他们的帖子。
  5. 用户可以关闭通知或点击它以打开应用程序并查看喜欢他们的帖子的用户。
  6. 如果用户点击通知并打开应用程序，他们可以看到喜欢他们帖子的用户的个人资料，并通过喜欢他们的帖子或关注他们的帐户来与他们互动。

现在我们知道我们在做什么，让我们将应用内通知集成到我们的应用中。

好吧……这不是先有鸡还是先有蛋的问题； 我们需要先吃鸡——我们的应用程序。

我最近开始尝试使用 Angular 并且越来越喜欢这个框架，但我不会试图说服你加入黑暗面。

随意访问已部署的版本： in-app-notification-with-angular.vercel ...

或者克隆这个repo

它应该是这样的：

创建一个 Angular 应用程序

先决条件：（你需要确保你没有掉头发的东西）

在我们开始之前，请确保您的系统上安装了以下内容：

  • 节点.js
  • 角度版本 > 0.15.0

如果您仍然需要安装这些工具，您可以从他们的官方网站下载并安装它们。

第 1 步：创建一个新的 Angular 应用程序

要创建新的 Angular 应用程序，请打开终端或命令提示符并运行以下命令：

在这里， my-app是您的新 Angular 应用程序的名称。 此命令将创建一个新的 Angular 应用程序，该应用程序具有基本文件结构并安装了所有必要的依赖项。

第二步：启动开发服务器

通过运行以下命令导航到应用程序目录：

进入应用程序目录后，您可以通过运行以下命令启动开发服务器：

此命令将启动开发服务器并在默认浏览器中启动您的应用程序。 您可以通过导航到http://localhost:4200/来访问您的应用程序。

第 3 步：修改应用程序

现在您的应用程序已启动并运行，您可以开始修改它以满足您的要求。 您可以通过编辑位于src目录中的文件来修改应用程序。

  • 您可以修改这些文件以添加新组件、更改布局以及向您的应用程序添加测试或新功能。

    注册 Novu

    我们会为我们的通知使用开箱即用的解决方案，因为我们很懒惰并且不断寻找生活中的捷径

    如果您过去曾尝试自己构建一个通知系统，那么在阅读本文之前，您很快就会爱上我。

    我们将使用 Novu，因为它是一个开源通知基础架构，使我们能够触发基于事件的通知工作流。

    第 1 步：注册

    单击此链接

    第 2 步：创建工作流程

    您可以按照入门指南或前往通知模板并创建您的模板（工作流程）

    添加通知中心

    第 1 步：安装

    在终端中，导航到 Angular 应用程序的根目录。

    运行以下命令生成新组件：

     npm install @novu/notification-center-angular or yarn add @novu/notification-center-angular

    第 2 步：添加 Novu 模块

    现在，导航到app.module.ts文件 (my-app/src/app/app.module.ts)

     // my-app/src/app/app.module.ts **** import { NgModule } from '@angular/core'; import { BrowserModule } from '@angular/platform-browser'; import { AppComponent } from './app.component'; @NgModule({ declarations: [ AppComponent ], imports: [ BrowserModule ], providers: [], bootstrap: [AppComponent] }) export class AppModule { }

    从'@angular/core'导入CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA 。

     // my-app/src/app/app.module.ts import { CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA, NgModule } from '@angular/core'; import { BrowserModule } from '@angular/platform-browser'; import { AppComponent } from './app.component'; @NgModule({ declarations: [ AppComponent ], imports: [ BrowserModule ], schemas: [CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA], providers: [], bootstrap: [AppComponent] }) export class AppModule { }

    添加Novu的通知中心模块

    // my-app/src/app/app.module.ts import { CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA, NgModule } from '@angular/core'; import { BrowserModule } from '@angular/platform-browser'; import { AppComponent } from './app.component'; import { NotificationCenterModule } from '@novu/notification-center-angular'; @NgModule({ declarations: [ AppComponent ], imports: [ BrowserModule, NotificationCenterModule ], schemas: [CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA], providers: [], bootstrap: [AppComponent] }) export class AppModule { }

    第三步：配置应用环境

    使用 Angular CLI，首先运行生成环境命令
    此处显示创建src/environments/目录并配置项目以使用这些文件。

导航到my-app/src/environments/environment.ts并添加以下变量： subscriberId 、 applicationIdentifier

 export const environment = { production: false, subscriberId: "", applicationIdentifier: "" };

我们的通知中心将向 Novu 的 API 发出GET请求，以实际将通知推送到提要中，这些变量是必需的。

现在我们需要添加这些变量：

可以在此处找到Application Identifier ： web.novu.co/settings （Novu 的设置部分）

现在将其添加到您的my-app/src/environments/environment.ts文件中

export const environment = { production: true, subscriberId: "<REPLACE_WITH_USER_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER>", applicationIdentifier: "<REPLACE_WITH_APP_ID_FROM_ADMIN_PANEL>" };

和my-app/src/environments/environment.development.ts文件

export const environment = { production: false, subscriberId: "<REPLACE_WITH_USER_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER>", applicationIdentifier: "<REPLACE_WITH_APP_ID_FROM_ADMIN_PANEL>" };

让我们前往my-app/src/app/app.component.ts文件

import { Component } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ selector: 'app-root', templateUrl: './app.component.html', styleUrls: ['./app.component.css'] }) export class AppComponent { title = 'my-app'; }

现在，我们将导入environment变量，使它们可以在app.component.html和通知中心的styles属性中访问（有很多属性，但稍后您可以发现它们）

 import { Component } from '@angular/core'; import { environment } from '../environments/environment'; @Component({ selector: 'app-root', templateUrl: './app.component.html', styleUrls: ['./app.component.css'] }) export class AppComponent { title = 'my-app'; subscriberId = environment.subscriberId; applicationIdentifier = environment.applicationIdentifier; styles = { bellButton: { root: { svg: { color: 'white', width: '45px', height: '40px', fill: 'white', }, }, dot: { rect: { fill: 'rgb(221, 0, 49)', strokeWidth: '0.2', stroke: 'white', width: '3.5px', height: '3.5px', }, left: '40%', }, }, header: { root: { backgroundColor: '', '&:hover': { backgroundColor: '' }, '.some_class': { color: '' }, }, }, layout: { root: { backgroundColor: '', }, }, popover: { arrow: { backgroundColor: '', border: '', }, }, }; sessionLoaded = (data: unknown) => { console.log('loaded', { data }); }; }

您可能会在[localhost:4200](http://localhost:4200)中看到一些错误，我们现在将修复它！

发生这种情况是因为 Angular 组件是作为原始 React 组件的包装器生成的。 这种方法很聪明，因为它允许 Novu 的工程师专注于以 React 方式创建和开发事物。 此外，许多其他框架仍然可以使用包装方法创建的组件。

我们需要添加@types/react作为开发依赖，以便角度组件正常工作。

打开终端并导航到应用程序根目录并键入以下内容：

 npm i @types/react or yarn add @types/react

现在前往my-app/tsconfig.json文件

/* To learn more about this file see: https://angular.io/config/tsconfig. */ { "compileOnSave": false, "compilerOptions": { "baseUrl": "./", "outDir": "./dist/out-tsc", "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true, "strict": true, "noImplicitOverride": true, "noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature": true, "noImplicitReturns": true, "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true, "sourceMap": true, "declaration": false, "downlevelIteration": true, "experimentalDecorators": true, "moduleResolution": "node", "importHelpers": true, "target": "ES2022", "module": "ES2022", "useDefineForClassFields": false, "lib": [ "ES2022", "dom" ] }, "angularCompilerOptions": { "enableI18nLegacyMessageIdFormat": false, "strictInjectionParameters": true, "strictInputAccessModifiers": true, "strictTemplates": true } }

我们将添加"allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true到compilerOptions数组。

 /* To learn more about this file see: https://angular.io/config/tsconfig. */ { "compileOnSave": false, "compilerOptions": { "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true, "baseUrl": "./", "outDir": "./dist/out-tsc", "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true, "strict": true, "noImplicitOverride": true, "noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature": true, "noImplicitReturns": true, "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true, "sourceMap": true, "declaration": false, "downlevelIteration": true, "experimentalDecorators": true, "moduleResolution": "node", "importHelpers": true, "target": "ES2022", "module": "ES2022", "useDefineForClassFields": false, "lib": [ "ES2022", "dom" ] }, "angularCompilerOptions": { "enableI18nLegacyMessageIdFormat": false, "strictInjectionParameters": true, "strictInputAccessModifiers": true, "strictTemplates": true } }

要查看已解决的错误，您可能需要转到运行该应用程序的终端并再次为该应用程序提供服务，以使compilerOptions更改生效。

第四步：添加通知中心组件

打开my-app/src/app/app.component.html文件。

该文件包含 CSS 代码和 HTML 代码——理想情况下，您应该将 CSS 分离到my-app/src/app/app.component.css文件中，但这不是强制性的。

我们将把我们的通知中心添加到 .toolbar div。

将以下内容粘贴到您的app.component.html文件中：

 <div id="bell-icon"> <notification-center-component [subscriberId]="subscriberId" [applicationIdentifier]="applicationIdentifier" [sessionLoaded]="sessionLoaded" [styles]="styles" ></notification-center-component> </div>

在<style>标签中，我们还想为我们的#bell-icon添加一些边距，以便它在其他图标旁边看起来不错。

 .toolbar #bell-icon { height: ''; margin: 0 16px; }

现在您应该会在应用程序的工具栏部分看到铃铛图标（通知中心）

测试通知中心

如果你失败了，那将是非常尴尬的，但不要让它气馁，并再次彻底检查前面的步骤，以确保你没有遗漏/跳过任何内容。

现在让我们测试我们的通知工作流程（模板）：

您应该会看到一个红点，表示您已收到通知。

就是这样！

这就是我们在本教程开头试图实现的目标。
如果您一直关注到这一部分并且您的应用程序正在运行，请拍拍自己的背。 恭喜你，你做得很好！

展望未来，我将在 Angular 中分享许多此类教程和应用程序，因此请确保您继续关注。

直到下一次，
皮尔斯曼

解除暂停后，novu 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，novu 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

This article explains how to add an in-app notification center to your Angular app and gives you a few reasons to smile 🙃

In one of the screenshots, there's a reference to one of my all-time favorite movies. If you can correctly guess which movie it is and leave your answer in the comments section, I'll make sure to acknowledge you in some way in my upcoming article. 🤓

Now that you're happy, we can start the tutorial.

Many notifications that may be convenient or necessary for your use case are available.

Here are some notification (communication) channels you are probably familiar with:

  • In-App
  • SMS
  • Emails
  • Chat (Discord, Slack, MS Teams, WhatsApp, Telegram)
  • Push

However, we will eat only 1/5 of this delicious pie - In-App notification. This communication channel allows you to interact with your users within your application.

Assuming you've not been living under a rock for the past decade, let's observe an example of an In-App event-based notification workflow for a social media app like Instagram:

  1. User opens the Instagram app and scrolls through their feed, looking at photos and videos posted by other users.
  2. The user posts a photo or video to their own Instagram profile.
  3. Another user sees the post and likes it.
  4. The Instagram app detects the like event and triggers a notification to the user who posted the content.
  5. The notification appears on the user's screen, notifying them that another user has liked their post.
  6. The user can either dismiss the notification or tap on it to open the app and view the user who liked their post.
  7. If the user taps on the notification and opens the app, they can see the profile of the user who liked their post and engages with them by liking their posts or following their account.

Now that we know what we're doing let's integrate In-App notifications into our app.

Well…It’s not a chicken and egg type of question; we need to have our chicken first - our app.

I recently started experimenting with Angular and have grown quite fond of this framework, but I will not be trying to convince you to join to the dark side.

Feel free to visit the deployed version: in-app-notification-with-angular.vercel…

Or clone this repo

That’s what it should look like:

Create an Angular app

Prerequisites: (The things you need to ensure you have not lost your hair)

Before we start, make sure you have the following installed on your system:

  • Node.js
  • NPM (Node Package Manager)
  • Angular CLI (Command Line Interface)
  • Angular version > 0.15.0

If you still need to install these tools, you can download and install them from their official websites.

Step 1: Creating a new Angular app

To create a new Angular app, open a terminal or command prompt and run the following command:

Here, my-app is the name of your new Angular app. This command will create a new Angular app with a basic file structure and all the necessary dependencies installed.

Step 2: Starting the development server

Navigate to the app directory by running the following command:

Once you are in the app directory, you can start the development server by running the following command:

This command will start the development server and launch your app in the default browser. You can access your app by navigating to http://localhost:4200/.

Step 3: Modifying the app

Now that your app is up and running, you can start modifying it to fit your requirements. You can modify the app by editing the files located in the src directory.

  • index.html: This file is the main entry point of your app.
  • app.component.ts: This file contains the main component of your app.
  • app.component.html: This file contains the HTML template for your app component.
  • app.component.css: This file contains the CSS styles for your app component.
  • app.component.spec.ts: This file contains unit tests for the App root.

You can modify these files to add new components, change the layout, and add tests or new functionality to your app.

Signing up for Novu

We will use an out-of-the-box solution for our notifications because we are lazy and constantly looking for shortcuts in life

(those millennials….)

If you tried to build a notification system by yourself in the past, before reading this, you will love me in a few moments.

We will be using Novu, as it’s an open-source notification infrastructure that enables us to trigger an event-based notification workflow.

Step 1: Sign Up

Click this link

Step 2: Creating A Workflow

You can follow the Get Started guide or head to notification templates and create your template (workflow)

Adding a notification center

Step 1: Installation

In the terminal, navigate to the root directory of your Angular application.

Run the following command to generate a new component:

npm install @novu/notification-center-angular

or

yarn add @novu/notification-center-angular

Step 2: Adding Novu Module

Now, navigate to the app.module.ts file (my-app/src/app/app.module.ts)

// my-app/src/app/app.module.ts
****
import { NgModule } from '@angular/core';
import { BrowserModule } from '@angular/platform-browser';
import { AppComponent } from './app.component'; @NgModule({ declarations: [ AppComponent ], imports: [ BrowserModule ], providers: [], bootstrap: [AppComponent]
})
export class AppModule { }

Import CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA from '@angular/core'.

// my-app/src/app/app.module.ts import { CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA, NgModule } from '@angular/core';
import { BrowserModule } from '@angular/platform-browser';
import { AppComponent } from './app.component'; @NgModule({ declarations: [ AppComponent ], imports: [ BrowserModule ], schemas: [CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA], providers: [], bootstrap: [AppComponent]
})
export class AppModule { }

Add Novu’s notification center module

// my-app/src/app/app.module.ts import { CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA, NgModule } from '@angular/core';
import { BrowserModule } from '@angular/platform-browser';
import { AppComponent } from './app.component';
import { NotificationCenterModule } from '@novu/notification-center-angular'; @NgModule({ declarations: [ AppComponent ], imports: [ BrowserModule, NotificationCenterModule ], schemas: [CUSTOM_ELEMENTS_SCHEMA], providers: [], bootstrap: [AppComponent]
})
export class AppModule { }

Step 3: Configuring Application Environments

Using the Angular CLI, start by running the generate environments command
 shown here to create the src/environments/  directory and configure the project to use these files.

Navigate to my-app/src/environments/environment.ts and add the following variables: subscriberId, applicationIdentifier

export const environment = { production: false, subscriberId: "", applicationIdentifier: ""
};

These variables are needed for the GET request our notification center will make to Novu’s API to actually push notifications into the feed.

Now we need to add those variables:

Application Identifier can be found here:web.novu.co/settings (Novu’s settings section)

**subscriberId** can be found here: web.novu.co/subscribers (Novu’s Subscribers section)

Now add it into your my-app/src/environments/environment.ts file

export const environment = { production: true, subscriberId: "<REPLACE_WITH_USER_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER>", applicationIdentifier: "<REPLACE_WITH_APP_ID_FROM_ADMIN_PANEL>"
};

and to the my-app/src/environments/environment.development.ts file

export const environment = { production: false, subscriberId: "<REPLACE_WITH_USER_UNIQUE_IDENTIFIER>", applicationIdentifier: "<REPLACE_WITH_APP_ID_FROM_ADMIN_PANEL>"
};

Let’s head to my-app/src/app/app.component.ts file

import { Component } from '@angular/core'; @Component({ selector: 'app-root', templateUrl: './app.component.html', styleUrls: ['./app.component.css']
})
export class AppComponent { title = 'my-app';
}

The app.component.ts file is a critical part of an Angular application, as it defines the root component and provides the foundation for the rest of the app's functionality.

Now, we are going to import the environment variables to make them accessible in the app.component.html and the styles properties of our notification center (there are many properties, but you can discover them later on)

import { Component } from '@angular/core';
import { environment } from '../environments/environment'; @Component({ selector: 'app-root', templateUrl: './app.component.html', styleUrls: ['./app.component.css']
})
export class AppComponent { title = 'my-app'; subscriberId = environment.subscriberId; applicationIdentifier = environment.applicationIdentifier; styles = { bellButton: { root: { svg: { color: 'white', width: '45px', height: '40px', fill: 'white', }, }, dot: { rect: { fill: 'rgb(221, 0, 49)', strokeWidth: '0.2', stroke: 'white', width: '3.5px', height: '3.5px', }, left: '40%', }, }, header: { root: { backgroundColor: '', '&:hover': { backgroundColor: '' }, '.some_class': { color: '' }, }, }, layout: { root: { backgroundColor: '', }, }, popover: { arrow: { backgroundColor: '', border: '', }, }, }; sessionLoaded = (data: unknown) => { console.log('loaded', { data }); };
}

You might see some errors in the [localhost:4200](http://localhost:4200) we will fix it now!

This occurs because the Angular component is generated as a wrapper around the original React component. This approach is clever, as it allows Novu's engineers to focus on creating and developing things in the React way. Additionally, many other frameworks can still use the created components using the wrapping approach.

We need to add @types/react as dev dependency for the angular component to work properly.

Open your terminal and navigate to the app root directory and type the following:

npm i @types/react

or

yarn add @types/react

Now head to the my-app/tsconfig.json file

/* To learn more about this file see: https://angular.io/config/tsconfig. */
{ "compileOnSave": false, "compilerOptions": { "baseUrl": "./", "outDir": "./dist/out-tsc", "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true, "strict": true, "noImplicitOverride": true, "noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature": true, "noImplicitReturns": true, "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true, "sourceMap": true, "declaration": false, "downlevelIteration": true, "experimentalDecorators": true, "moduleResolution": "node", "importHelpers": true, "target": "ES2022", "module": "ES2022", "useDefineForClassFields": false, "lib": [ "ES2022", "dom" ] }, "angularCompilerOptions": { "enableI18nLegacyMessageIdFormat": false, "strictInjectionParameters": true, "strictInputAccessModifiers": true, "strictTemplates": true }
}

And we’re going to add "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true to the compilerOptions array.

/* To learn more about this file see: https://angular.io/config/tsconfig. */
{ "compileOnSave": false, "compilerOptions": { "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true, "baseUrl": "./", "outDir": "./dist/out-tsc", "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true, "strict": true, "noImplicitOverride": true, "noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature": true, "noImplicitReturns": true, "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true, "sourceMap": true, "declaration": false, "downlevelIteration": true, "experimentalDecorators": true, "moduleResolution": "node", "importHelpers": true, "target": "ES2022", "module": "ES2022", "useDefineForClassFields": false, "lib": [ "ES2022", "dom" ] }, "angularCompilerOptions": { "enableI18nLegacyMessageIdFormat": false, "strictInjectionParameters": true, "strictInputAccessModifiers": true, "strictTemplates": true }
}

To see the error resolved, you might need to go to the terminal that is running the app and serve the app again for compilerOptions changes to take effect.

Step 4: Add the notification center component

Open the my-app/src/app/app.component.html file.

This file contain the CSS code along with the HTML one - ideally you should separate the CSS to the my-app/src/app/app.component.css file, but it’s not mandatory.

We will add our notification center to the .toolbar div.

Paste the following into your app.component.html file:

 <div id="bell-icon"> <notification-center-component [subscriberId]="subscriberId" [applicationIdentifier]="applicationIdentifier" [sessionLoaded]="sessionLoaded" [styles]="styles" ></notification-center-component> </div>

The div should look like this:

<!-- Toolbar -->
<div class="toolbar" role="banner"> <img width="40" alt="Angular Logo" src="<TOO LONG FOR DEV.TO MARKDOWN TO PROCESS>" /> <span>Welcome</span> <div class="spacer"></div> <div id="bell-icon"> <notification-center-component [subscriberId]="subscriberId" [applicationIdentifier]="applicationIdentifier" [sessionLoaded]="sessionLoaded" [styles]="styles" ></notification-center-component> </div> <a aria-label="Angular on twitter" target="_blank" rel="noopener" href="https://twitter.com/angular" title="Twitter"> <svg id="twitter-logo" height="24" data-name="Logo" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 400 400"> <rect width="400" height="400" fill="none"/> <path d="<TOO LONG FOR DEV.TO MARKDOWN TO PROCESS>" fill="#fff"/> </svg> </a> <a aria-label="Angular on YouTube" target="_blank" rel="noopener" href="https://youtube.com/angular" title="YouTube"> <svg id="youtube-logo" height="24" width="24" data-name="Logo" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="#fff"> <path d="M0 0h24v24H0V0z" fill="none"/> <path d="<TOO LONG FOR DEV.TO MARKDOWN TO PROCESS>"/> </svg> </a>
</div>

And in the <style> tag, we also want to add some margin to our #bell-icon so that it looks good next to the other icons.

.toolbar #bell-icon { height: ''; margin: 0 16px;
}

And now you should see the bell icon (the notification center) in the toolbar section of your app

Test the notification center

It’s going to be very awkward if you will fail, but don’t let it discourage you and go over the previous steps one more time thoroughly to make sure you haven’t missed/skipped anything.

Now let’s test our notification workflow (template):

You should see a red dot indicating that you have received a notification.

And that’s it!

This is what we’d sought to achieve at the beginning of this tutorial.
If you’ve followed till this part and your app is working, pat yourself on the back. Congratulations, you did well!

Going forward, I’ll be sharing many such tutorials and apps in Angular so make sure you stay tuned for that.

Until next time,
Pearceman

Items.Title: 使用预加载策略优化 Angular 应用程序的用户体验 | Optimize your Angular app's user experience with preloading strategies
Items.Description:
性能优化是创建成功的 Angular 应用程序的一个关键方面。 缓慢的加载时间会导致令人沮丧的用户体验，并最终将用户赶走。 一种提高性能的常用技术是延迟加载，它允许应用程序被分解成更小的块，可以按需加载。

然而，另一个经常被忽视的重要概念是预加载策略。

在“少加载，多做：Angular 预加载策略指南”系列的第一篇文章中，我们将探索预加载策略的世界，以及如何使用它们来加快加载过程并提供更流畅的用户体验。 我们将讨论什么是预加载策略、它们如何工作以及它们提供的好处。 为了帮助说明这些概念，我们将通过一个示例来说明如何创建自定义预加载策略。 到本文结束时，您将对预加载策略以及如何将它们应用于您的 Angular 应用程序有深入的了解。

目录

为什么性能优化很重要

在创建网页或在线工具时，性能优化是提供良好用户体验（通常称为“UX”）的关键方面。

您还记得上次等待网页加载是什么时候吗？ 无论该站点可能是什么，我怀疑您是否记得它是一次愉快的体验。

一般来说，没有人喜欢使用速度慢的网站，因为它会导致浏览它的用户感到沮丧甚至恐惧（“它会正确地保存我的进度吗？”，“我可以用我的个人信息信任这个网站吗？”）。

确保网站平稳运行并快速加载可以带来更好的整体用户体验。

此外，应用程序的速度会影响用户对您品牌的看法：缓慢且反应迟钝的网站会产生负面印象，使用户不太可能返回。

为什么预加载策略很重要？

在寻求提高 Angular 应用程序的性能时，您听到的第一件事就是延迟加载。

通过拆分您的应用程序模块将功能分成更小的块，您可以动态加载它们，而不是将它们捆绑在一起，并强制用户在访问任何页面时下载整个网站。

然而，有时，您可能知道，从业务角度来看，用户在访问页面时会使用一些惰性模块。 在这种情况下，预加载它们可能是有意义的，以避免对最终想要查看页面的用户造成不必要的延迟。

对于这些特定情况，Angular 引入了预加载策略的概念。

预加载策略是一种告诉 Angular 的路由器在后台加载模块的方法，这样当用户导航到新页面时，所需的模块已经加载并且页面几乎是瞬间出现的。

预加载策略是一个简单的 Angular 类，它扩展了定义如下的PreloadingStrategy抽象类：

 abstract class PreloadingStrategy { abstract preload(route: Route, fn: () => Observable<any>): Observable<any> }

执行时，如果应该预加载route ，此方法将返回对fn调用，否则返回Observable<null> 。

默认情况下，当使用延迟加载时，所有模块都被延迟加载。

这是通过默认启用NoPreloading策略来实现的，它被描述为：

提供不预加载任何模块的预加载策略。

它的使用将导致仅在请求时加载目标模块：

不幸的是，这会导致用户在应用程序中导航时的后续加载时间变慢。

PreloadAllModules

它的对面也存在PreloadAllModules策略，顾名思义，它将预加载所有内容：

提供预加载策略，尽可能快地预加载所有模块。

与之前的策略不同，这次所有内容都是一次性加载的：

但是，如果您想更好地控制加载 Angular 应用程序的方式，那么这些策略都可能不是最佳选择。

对于这些情况，可以创建和使用您自己的预加载策略。

使用预加载策略

为了指示路由器应应用哪种预加载策略，您需要将其提供给应用程序根模块中RouterModule.forRoot()方法的preloadingStrategy属性：

 @NgModule({ imports: [RouterModule.forRoot(routes, { preloadingStrategy: /* ... */ })], }) export class AppModule { }

但是，如果您现在正在使用Standalone Components ，您可能希望在应用程序引导期间使用提供程序来调用。

这可以通过将withPreloading添加到provideRouter中完成的设置来完成：

 bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, { providers: [ provideRouter(routes, withPreloading(/* ... */)) ], });

请注意，如果您自己编写预加载策略的实现，您也必须在根级别提供它，以便可以从依赖注入容器中检索它。

实施我们自己的预加载策略

我们曾经提到过创建自定义预加载策略的重要性，让我们看看如何去做。

目的

我们的自定义预加载策略将简单地预加载任何在其路由数据中将标志preload设置为true延迟加载路由。

设置

我将稍微修改我在之前的 GIF 中定义的路由以添加标志：

 export const routes: Route[] = [ { path: "feature-1", loadComponent: () => import("./app/feature-1/feature-1.component").then( (m) => m.Feature1Component ), // 👇 This route should be preloaded data: { preload: true }, }, { path: "feature-2", loadChildren: () => import("./app/feature-2/feature-2.route").then( (m) => m.routes ), }, ];

使用我们的自定义PreloadingStrategy ，我们应该看到“Feature #1”从一开始就加载了，但“Feature #2”没有。

给我看代码！

预加载策略只不过是扩展PreloadingStrategy服务。

了解这一点，让我们创建我们的FlagBasedPreloadingStrategy服务：

 // flag-based.preloading-strategy.ts import { Injectable } from "@angular/core"; import { PreloadingStrategy, Route } from "@angular/router"; import { Observable } from "rxjs"; @Injectable({ providedIn: "root" }) export class FlagBasedPreloadingStrategy extends PreloadingStrategy { // 👇 For clarity, I prefer `load` rather than `fn` for the callback name preload(route: Route, load: () => Observable<any>): Observable<any> { return route.data?.["preload"] === true ? load() : of(null); } }

请注意我们是如何在root中提供它的，以便我们可以指示路由器在引导程序中使用它

逻辑在这里并不重要。 我们只需检查当前路由的标志，如果存在，我们将加载模块，否则我们不会。

看起来很棒！ 我们仍然必须指示路由器才能使用它。 这可以通过与内置方法相同的方式完成：通过在引导阶段提供它：

 bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, { providers: [ provideRouter(routes, withPreloading(FlagBasedPreloadingStrategy)) ], });

现在，如果我们在 devtools 中打开网络选项卡，我们应该看到功能 #1 已加载但功能 #2 未加载：

同样，如果我们现在标记功能 #2 而不是功能 #1，我们应该看到它被加载而不是另一个：

添加标志：

 export const routes: Route[] = [ { path: "feature-1", loadComponent: () => import("./app/feature-1/feature-1.component").then( (m) => m.Feature1Component ), - data: { preload: true }, }, { path: "feature-2", loadChildren: () => import("./app/feature-2/feature-2.route").then( (m) => m.routes ), + data: { preload: true }, }, ];

按预期工作！

外卖

在本文中，我们了解了什么是预加载策略，以及它们如何帮助您优化应用程序的加载，通过在后台加载所需的模块来提供更流畅的用户体验，这样用户就不必等待它们加载导航到新页面时加载。

默认情况下，Angular 提供两种策略：

但是，在某些业务案例中，您可能希望以特定方式编排惰性模块的加载。 对于这种情况，实施自定义预加载策略可能是解决方案

如果您想检查生成的代码，可以前往相关的


在本系列的后续文章中，我们将看到如何阐明守卫和预加载策略，敬请期待！

我希望你在那里学到了一些有用的东西！

原文:

Performance optimization is a critical aspect of creating a successful Angular app. Slow loading times can lead to a frustrating user experience and ultimately drive users away. One common technique to improve performance is lazy loading, which allows the app to be broken down into smaller chunks that can be loaded on demand.

However, another important concept that is often overlooked is preloading strategies.

In this first article of the series "Load Less, Do More: A Guide to Angular Preloading Strategies", we will explore the world of preloading strategies and how they can be used to speed up the loading process and provide a smoother user experience. We will discuss what preloading strategies are, how they work, and the benefits they offer. To help illustrate these concepts, we will walk through an example of how to create a custom preloading strategy. By the end of this article, you will have a solid understanding of preloading strategies and how they can be applied to your Angular app.

Table of Contents

Why performance optimization is important

When creating web pages or online tools, performance optimization is a crucial aspect to provide a decent user experience, often referred to as "UX".

Do you remember the last time you were waiting for a web page to load? No matter what the site might have been, I doubt that you are remembering it as a pleasant experience.

In general, nobody likes to use a slow website as it can lead to frustration or even fear from the users browsing it ("Will it correctly saves my progress?", "Can I trust this website with my personal information?").

Ensuring that a website runs smoothly and loads quickly leads to a better UX overall.

Moreover, the speed of the app can impact how users perceive your brand: a slow and unresponsive site can create a negative impression, making users less likely to return.

Why do preloading strategies matter?

One of the first thing you fill hear about when seeking to improve the performances of your Angular app is lazy loading.

By splitting your application modules features into smaller chunks, you can load them on the fly instead of bundling them all together and force the user to download the whole website when accessing any page.

However, sometimes, you might know that, from a business perspective, a user will use some lazy modules when accessing a page. In that case, preloading them might make sense, to avoid an unnecessary latency to the user that will eventually want to see the page.

For those specific cases, Angular introduced the concept of preloading strategies.

A preloading strategy is a way to tell the Angular's router to load modules in the background, so that when the user navigates to a new page, the required modules have already been loaded and the page appears almost instantaneously.

A preloading strategy is a simple Angular class that extends the PreloadingStrategy abstract class defined as such:

abstract class PreloadingStrategy { abstract preload(route: Route, fn: () => Observable<any>): Observable<any>
}

When executed, this methods will return the call to fn if the route should be preloaded, or an Observable<null> if not.

Default preloading strategies in Angular

NoPreloading

By default, when using lazy loading, all modules are lazily loaded.

This is achieve by enabling the NoPreloading Strategy by default, which is described as:

Provides a preloading strategy that does not preload any modules.

Its usage will result in the loading of the targeted module only when requested:

Unfortunately, this can results in slower subsequent loading time as the user navigates through the app.

PreloadAllModules

It's opposite also exists as the PreloadAllModules Strategy which will, as its name suggests it, preload everything:

Provides a preloading strategy that preloads all modules as quickly as possible.

Unlike the previous strategy, this time everything is loaded at once:

However, neither of those strategy might prove optimal if you want finer control on the way you load your Angular app.

For those cases, it is possible to create and use your own preloading strategy.

Using Preloading Strategies

In order to indicate which preloading strategy the router should apply, you need to provide it to the preloadingStrategy property of the RouterModule.forRoot() method in your app's root module:

@NgModule({ imports: [RouterModule.forRoot(routes, { preloadingStrategy: /* ... */ })],
})
export class AppModule { }

However, if you are now using Standalone Components, you might want to use a provider to call during the bootstrap of your application.

This can be done by adding withPreloading to the setup done in provideRouter:

bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, { providers: [ provideRouter(routes, withPreloading(/* ... */)) ],
});

Note that if you write your own implementation of a preloading strategy, you will have to provide it on the root level too, so that it can be retrieved from the dependency injection container.

Implementing our own preloading strategy

We mentioned the importance of creating a custom preloading strategy sometime, let's see how to do it.

Purpose

Our custom preloading strategy will simply preload any lazy loaded route that has the flag preload set to true in its route data.

Setup

I will slightly modify the routes that I defined in the previous GIFs to add the flag:

export const routes: Route[] = [ { path: "feature-1", loadComponent: () => import("./app/feature-1/feature-1.component").then( (m) => m.Feature1Component ), // 👇 This route should be preloaded data: { preload: true }, }, { path: "feature-2", loadChildren: () => import("./app/feature-2/feature-2.route").then( (m) => m.routes ), },
];

With our custom PreloadingStrategy, we should see that the "Feature #1" is loaded from the start but "Feature #2" is not.

Show me the code!

A preloading strategy is nothing more than a service that extends PreloadingStrategy.

Knowing this, let's create our FlagBasedPreloadingStrategy service:

// flag-based.preloading-strategy.ts
import { Injectable } from "@angular/core";
import { PreloadingStrategy, Route } from "@angular/router"; import { Observable } from "rxjs"; @Injectable({ providedIn: "root" })
export class FlagBasedPreloadingStrategy extends PreloadingStrategy { // 👇 For clarity, I prefer `load` rather than `fn` for the callback name preload(route: Route, load: () => Observable<any>): Observable<any> { return route.data?.["preload"] === true ? load() : of(null); }
}

Notice how we provided it in root so that we can indicate the router to use it on bootstrap

The logic doesn't matter much here. We simply check the current route for the flag and, if present, we will load the module and otherwise we will not.

Looking great! We still have to indicate the router to use it tho. This can be done in the same way as the built in ones: by providing it during the bootstrap phase:

bootstrapApplication(AppComponent, { providers: [ provideRouter(routes, withPreloading(FlagBasedPreloadingStrategy)) ],
});

Now, if we open the network tab in the devtools, we should see that the feature #1 is loaded but feature #2 is not:

Similarly, if we now flag the feature #2 instead of the feature #1, we should see it loaded instead of the other:

to add the flag:

export const routes: Route[] = [
 { path: "feature-1", loadComponent: () => import("./app/feature-1/feature-1.component").then( (m) => m.Feature1Component ),
- data: { preload: true },
 }, { path: "feature-2", loadChildren: () => import("./app/feature-2/feature-2.route").then( (m) => m.routes ),
+ data: { preload: true },
 },
];

Working as expected!

Takeaways

In this article, we saw what preloading strategies are and how they can help you to optimize the loading of your application to offer a smoother user experience by loading the required modules in the background, so the user doesn't have to wait for them to load when navigating to a new page.

By default, Angular provides two strategies:

However, in some business cases, you might want to orchestrate the loading of your lazy modules in a specific way. For such situations, implementing a custom preloading strategy might be the solution

If you would like to check the resulting code, you can head on to the associated


In a future article on this series, we will see how to articulate guards and preloading strategies, stay tuned!

I hope that you learnt something useful there!

Items.Title: 宣布美杜莎的社区出版物 | Announcing Medusa’s Community Publication
Items.Description:
在 Medusa，我们将推出我们的新社区出版物Medusa Community ，托管在 Hashnode 上。 该出版物为有兴趣撰写有关美杜莎的作家提供了一个平台，他们可以在这个平台上获得认可并为其作品付费。

继续阅读下文以了解有关此出版物的更多信息以及如何加入！

为什么要创建社区出版物？

去年，我们在美杜莎创建了一个作家计划，在那里我们能够与 200 多位作家合作制作与美杜莎相关的内容。 这使我们能够根据社区作者提供的不同技能来创建不同类型和级别的多样化内容。

随着 Writers Program 不断发展壮大并引起人们的兴趣，我们意识到突出社区工作并让更多人更容易做出贡献和加入的重要性。 社区出版物旨在解决我们在 Writers Program 中遇到的许多限制，同时还由社区为社区提供创意和有用的内容。

它是如何工作的？

我们在新的Write for Us 页面中分享有关新出版物的所有详细信息。 把它们加起来：

  • 您可以使用此表格作为作家加入。
  • 然后您将收到一封电子邮件，其中包含加入 Hashnode 出版物和任何其他必要资源的详细信息。
  • 就是这样！ 然后您可以开始编写内容并将其提交到社区出版物。

重要笔记

  • 使用上述表格再次注册为作家非常重要，即使您之前是作家计划的成员。
  • 您的文章必须符合我们的写作指南。
  • 文章不会立即发布在出版物上，因为我们可能会指定一个时间表，说明我们决定发布文章的方式和时间。
  • 如果您的文章发表或排期，您将收到付款。
  • 虽然您通常可以写任何与美杜莎相关的内容，但我们也有关于我们反对哪些主题的指南以及写作时要记住的一些事项。 您可以在此处了解更多信息。

加入社区出版物的好处

  • 您的内容将被美杜莎社区认可。 我们还会经常在我们的渠道中分享我们认为有见地或有创意的文章。
  • 你的工作会得到报酬。 如果您的文章在 Hashnode 或其他出版物上有特色，我们还会提供奖金。

开始为美杜莎写作

对社区出版物感兴趣？ 您可以通过此表格加入出版物。

查看 Medusa 社区出版物并在 Hashnode 上关注它以了解所有社区内容的最新信息。

原文:

At Medusa, we’re introducing our new community publication, Medusa Community, hosted on Hashnode. This publication gives writers interested in writing about Medusa a platform where they can be recognized and paid for their work.

Keep reading below to learn more about this publication and how you can join!

Why create a community publication?

Last year, we created a Writers Program at Medusa, where we were able to work with over 200 writers in producing content related to Medusa. This has allowed us to create diverse content of different types and levels based on the different skills the community writers provided.

As the Writers Program continued to grow and gain interest, we realized how important it is to highlight the community’s work and make it easier for more people to contribute and join. The community publication aims to solve a lot of the limitations we had with the Writers Program, while also providing creative and useful content for the community, by the community.

How does it work?

We share all the details about the new publication in our new Write for Us page. To sum it up:

  • You can join as a writer using this form.
  • You’ll then receive an email with the details to join the Hashnode publication and any other necessary resources.
  • That’s it! You can then start writing and submitting content to the community publication.

Important notes

  • It’s important to sign up again as a writer using the form mentioned above, even if you were previously part of the Writers Program.
  • Your article must match our writing guidelines.
  • Articles are not instantly published on the publication, as we might specify a schedule on how and when we decide to post the article.
  • You’ll receive payment if your article is published or scheduled.
  • Although you can generally write about anything related to Medusa, we also have guidelines on what topics we are against and some things to keep in mind when writing. You can learn more here.

Benefits of joining the Community Publication

  • Your content will be recognized by the Medusa community. We’ll also often share articles we find insightful or creative across our channels.
  • You’ll get paid for your work. We also offer bonus payments if your article gets featured on Hashnode or other publications.

Start writing for Medusa

Interested in the Community Publication? You can join the publication through this form.

Check out the Medusa Community Publication and follow it on Hashnode to stay updated with all community content.

Once suspended, medusajs will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, medusajs will be able to comment and publish posts again.

If medusajs is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Hello, DEVs I created a javaScript function for fluid navBar but, it doesn't work because javascript does not recognize the section tag, no errors in console. here is my javaScript code below:

// Get all the sections on the page
const sections = document.querySelectorAll("section");

// Get the bubble element
const bubble = document.querySelector(".bubble");

// Define an array of gradients to be used for the bubble
const gradient = [
  "linear-gradient(to right top, #ffb88c, #de6262)",
  "linear-gradient(to right top, #a73737, #7a2828)",
  "linear-gradient(to right top, #ed4264, #ffedbc)",
];

// Define the options for the IntersectionObserver
const options = {
  threshold: 0.7,
};

// Create an IntersectionObserver instance and pass the callback function and options
let observer = new IntersectionObserver(navCheck, options);

// The callback function that gets called when an intersection occurs
function navCheck(entries) {
  // Loop through all the entries
  entries.forEach((entry) => {
    // Get the class name of the section
    const className = entry.target.className;

    // Get the active anchor with a matching data-page attribute
    const activeAnchor = document.querySelector(`[data-page=${className}]`);

    // Get the gradient index from the data-index attribute
    const gradientIndex = parseInt(entry.target.getAttribute("data-index"));

    // Get the bounding rectangle of the active anchor
    const coords = activeAnchor.getBoundingClientRect();

    // Create an object containing the dimensions and position of the active anchor
    const directions = {
      height: coords.height,
      top: coords.top,
      width: coords.width,
      left: coords.left,
    };

    // If the section is currently intersecting with the viewport
    if (entry.isIntersecting) {
      // Set the background of the bubble to the gradient corresponding to the current section
      bubble.style.background = gradient[gradientIndex];

      // Set the position and dimensions of the bubble to match the active anchor
      bubble.style.setProperty("left", `${directions.left}px`);
      bubble.style.setProperty("top", `${directions.top}px`);
      bubble.style.setProperty("width", `${directions.width}px`);
      bubble.style.setProperty("height", `${directions.height}px`);
    }
  });
}

// Observe all the sections on the page
sections.forEach((section) => {
  observer.observe(section);
});

for context, I am trying to match the (nav) activeAnchor background color to the active section background color, i.e. if in the home section nav home anchor should be the same color as the home section.

Items.Title: 从零到前端英雄：2023 年给初学者的提示 | From Zero to Frontend Hero: Tips for Beginner Developers in 2023
Items.Description:
前端开发是一个超级酷且不断变化的领域。 由于出现了所有新技术，它对于制作现代网络应用程序至关重要。 如果您是新手或想要改变现状的专业人士，学习前端开发是明智之举。 但说实话，开始可能很难。 这就是为什么我在这里为您提供 2023 年的一些提示。我将介绍基础知识、最新趋势和工具，以及如何克服常见挑战。 在我的帮助下，您将成为前端英雄并对自己的技能充满信心。 让我们开始吧！

了解前端开发的基础知识

前端开发可能会让新手望而生畏。 然而，通过正确的方法和基本的理解，任何人都可以立即成为前端英雄！

前端开发的基本概念

前端开发与用户交互的网站或应用程序部分有关。 它需要设计网站用户界面的风格、外观和操作。 在这个领域，了解用户体验和用户界面设计的概念至关重要。

前端开发建立在 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 的基础上。 响应式设计

构建跨多种设备和屏幕尺寸流畅运行的网站的概念被称为响应式设计。 随着移动设备的日益普及，响应式设计比以往任何时候都更加重要。

为前端开发选择合适的工具

如果您想成为一名成功的前端开发人员，那么为任务选择正确的工具至关重要。

代码编辑器

代码编辑器是用于编写和编辑代码的工具。 因为每个编辑器都有自己的一套功能和插件，所以选择最适合您的编辑器至关重要。

框架和库

提供预构建组件和功能的框架和库可以帮助加速开发过程。 浏览器开发者工具

浏览器开发人员工具是内置的 Web 浏览器功能，使开发人员能够分析和调试他们的代码。 这些工具对于前端开发人员用于识别和纠正代码中的错误至关重要。

采用有效的学习策略

前端开发需要承诺和实践。 这里有一些学习手艺的有效方法。

设定明确的目标

明确目标对于良好的学习至关重要。 设定精确的目标，例如学习新的 CSS 布局方法或创建响应式网站，将帮助您保持动力并走上正轨。

制定学习计划

学习计划可以帮助您保持井井有条并朝着目标前进。 将您的学习分成更小、更易于管理的步骤，并留出固定时间进行练习和复习。

定期练习

一致的实践对于培养前端开发能力至关重要。 每天或每周抽出时间来完成项目并磨练你的才能。

建立真实世界的项目和投资组合

创建专业的作品集和从事现实世界的项目是成为前端英雄的关键阶段。

确定正确的项目

选择一个符合您的兴趣和技能水平的项目。 创建一个您热衷的项目将帮助您保持积极性和参与度。

使用开源项目

使用开源项目可以帮助您获得专业知识并为前端开发人员社区做出贡献。 这是向更有经验的开发人员学习同时扩展您的产品组合的绝佳方法。

创建一个专业的投资组合

任何前端开发人员都应该拥有专业的作品集。 展示您最伟大的作品，概述您的方法和工具，并强调任何开源贡献。 精心设计的作品集将帮助您在潜在雇主和客户面前脱颖而出。

与其他前端开发人员协作和联网

与该领域的人合作和建立网络是作为前端开发人员取得进步的最佳方法之一。 通过与其他开发人员合作，您可以获得新方法并获得对最佳实践的有用见解。 以下是作为前端开发人员一起工作和建立网络的一些建议：

加入在线社区

论坛、聊天组和 Slack 频道等在线网络可能是结识其他开发人员的绝佳场所。 通过加入这些小组，您将能够提出问题、讨论想法并获得对您工作的反馈。 为开源项目做贡献

在提高能力的同时与其他开发人员合作的另一种绝妙方法是为开源项目做出贡献。 您可以通过为开源项目做出贡献来获得真实的经验并扩展您的简历。 可以在 GitHub 和 GitLab 等网站上找到要贡献的开源项目。

参加会议和聚会

亲自参加会议是与其他前端开发人员建立联系的绝佳方法。 这些活动提供了向行业专业人士学习、参加研讨会以及与其他开发人员交流的机会。 紧跟最新趋势和技术

对于前端开发人员来说，跟上最新的趋势和技术是至关重要的。 这有助于您保持竞争力，同时对您采用的技术和框架做出有根据的判断。 以下是一些可帮助您保持最新状态的提示：

跟随行业领导者

在 Twitter 和 LinkedIn 等社交媒体网站上关注行业专家是保持更新的最佳方法之一。 这些行业专业人士经常提供有关最新发展和创新的信息和想法。

阅读相关博客和文章

阅读前端开发博客和出版物也可以帮助您保持最新。 探索新技术

最后，定期调查新技术和框架至关重要。 尝试新的工具和技术将帮助您掌握它们的优势和局限性，以及哪些工具最适合特定任务。

克服前端开发初学者面临的常见挑战

开始前端开发的职业生涯可能很困难，但有一些策略可以克服典型的挑战。 以下是克服典型障碍的一些建议：

应对冒名顶替综合症

新开发人员经常会遇到冒名顶替综合症。 要克服它，不要将自己与他人进行比较，而要尝试专注于自己的成长和成功。 请记住，每个人都必须从某个地方开始。

管理时间和保持动力

当您刚开始作为前端开发人员时，可能很难管理您的时间并保持积极性。 为自己设定可实现的目标，并将较大的工作分成较小的活动以保持动力。 考虑使用 Trello 或 Asana 等时间管理软件。

使用遗留代码

使用旧代码可能会令人恐惧，但这是前端开发人员必须学习的一项技能。 在进行任何修改之前，请尝试掌握代码的用途和结构。 您还可以使用 GitHub 的拉取请求功能等技术与其他开发人员合作。

获得信心并提升您作为前端开发人员的职业生涯

一旦您获得了前端开发人员的经验，获得自信并推动您的职业发展至关重要。 以下是一些建议：

建立强大的在线形象

创建强大的在线形象可能会帮助您展示您的工作并与可能的雇主建立联系。 考虑开发个人网站并通过 Twitter 和 LinkedIn 等社交媒体渠道发布您的作品。

申请工作和导航面试

在申请前端开发工作时，请务必突出您的相关经验和技能。 准备分享您过去如何处理特定问题，并在面试中展示您解决问题的能力。

继续你的教育和职业发展

最后，保持你的教育和专业发展对你的进步至关重要。 要学习新技能并在该领域保持最新状态，请考虑参加研讨会、参加课程或获得认证。 结论

所以，事情是这样的：成为一名前端英雄需要大量的奉献、努力工作，并且始终乐于学习。 如果您遵循本文中的建议，您将走上成为一名成功的前端开发人员的正确轨道。 要变得更好，请保持好奇心、定期练习并与其他开发人员合作。 无论您是刚刚起步还是希望改变职业，前端开发都有无限可能。 我希望这篇文章能给你一些好的想法和动力去追逐你的梦想，成为一名前端英雄。

常见问题

前端开发是创建用户与之交互的网站或 Web 应用程序的一部分。 这包括用户界面的设计、布局和功能，以及实现它所需的编码语言和技术。

我需要计算机科学学位才能成为前端开发人员吗？

不，作为前端开发人员不需要计算机科学学位。 许多成功的前端开发人员通过在线课程、训练营或自学来学习他们的技能。 但是，扎实的计算机科学概念基础有助于理解前端开发的技术方面。

前端开发人员最重要的技能是什么？

前端开发人员必须精通 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript，并了解主要的前端框架和库。 强大的问题解决能力、对细节的关注和出色的沟通技巧对于每个开发人员来说也是必不可少的。

我怎样才能跟上前端开发的最新趋势和技术？

跟上前端开发的最新趋势和技术需要不断的研究和实验。 在社交媒体上关注行业专家和阅读相关博客和文章是保持更新的两种有效方式。 参加大会和会议，以及与其他开发人员合作，也可能提供重要的见解和了解该领域当前进展的机会。

感谢您的阅读！
Soumyadeep Mandal @imsampro Soumyadeep Mandal @imsampro



原文:

Frontend development is a super cool and always-changing field. Thanks to all the new technology out there, it's critical for making modern web apps. If you're a newbie or a pro looking to switch things up, learning frontend development is a smart move. But let's be real, it can be tough to get started. That's why I'm here to help you out with some tips for 2023. I'll go over the basics, the newest trends and tools, and how to overcome common challenges. With my help, you'll become a frontend hero and feel confident in your skills. So let's get started!

Understanding the Fundamentals of Frontend Development

Frontend development might appear intimidating to newcomers. However, with the right approach and fundamental understanding, anyone can become a frontend hero in no time!

Basic Concepts About Frontend Development

Frontend development is concerned with the portion of a website or application with which the user interacts. It entails designing the style, look, and operation of a website's user interface. In this sector, understanding the concepts of user experience and user interface design is critical.

HTML, CSS and JavaScript

Frontend development is built on the foundations of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. HTML is in charge of a webpage's structure and content, whereas CSS is in charge of style and layout. JavaScript is used to enhance the website's interactivity and functionality.

Responsive Design

The concept of building websites that perform fluidly across multiple devices and screen sizes is known as responsive design. With the growing popularity of mobile devices, responsive design is more vital than ever.

Choosing the Right Tools for Frontend Development

It's critical to pick the correct tools for the task if you want to be a successful frontend developer.

Code Editors

Code editors are tools for writing and editing code. Visual Studio Code, Sublime Text, and Atom are all popular code editors. Because each editor has its own set of features and plugins, it's critical to select the editor that works best for you.

Frameworks and Libraries

Frameworks and libraries, which provide pre-built components and functionality, can help to accelerate the development process. React and Angular are prominent frontend frameworks, whereas jQuery and Bootstrap are popular libraries.

Browser Developer Tools

Browser developer tools are built-in web browser features that enable developers to analyze and debug their code. These tools are critical for frontend developers to use in identifying and correcting errors in their code.

Adopting Effective Learning Strategies

Frontend development needs commitment and practice. Here are some effective methods for learning the craft.

Setting Clear Goals

Having defined goals and objectives is essential for good learning. Setting precise goals, such as learning a new CSS layout method or creating a responsive website, will help you stay motivated and on track.

Creating a Learning Plan

A learning plan can assist you in staying organized and on track with your objectives. Divide your learning into smaller, more manageable steps, and set aside regular time for practice and review.

Practicing Regularly

Consistent practice is essential for developing your frontend development abilities. Make time each day or week to work on projects and hone your talents.

Building Real-World Projects and Portfolios

Creating a professional portfolio and working on real-world projects are critical stages towards becoming a frontend hero.

Identifying the Right Project

Choose a project that corresponds to your interests and skill level. Creating a project that you are enthusiastic about will help you stay motivated and involved.

Working with Open Source Projects

Working with open source projects can help you acquire expertise and contribute to the community of frontend developers. It's an excellent method to learn from more experienced developers while also expanding your portfolio.

Creating a Professional Portfolio

Any frontend developer should have a professional portfolio. Showcase your greatest work, outline your method and tools, and emphasize any open source contributions. A well-crafted portfolio will assist you in standing out to prospective employers and clients.

Collaborating and Networking with Other Frontend Developers

Collaboration and networking with people in the field is one of the finest methods to advance as a frontend developer. You can acquire new approaches and receive useful insights into best practices by collaborating with other developers. Here are some pointers for working together and networking as a frontend developer:

Joining Online Communities

Online networks like forums, chat groups, and Slack channels may be fantastic locations to meet other developers. By joining these groups, you will be able to ask questions, discuss ideas, and receive feedback on your work. Reddit's r/Frontend and the Frontend Masters group are both excellent places to begin.

Contributing to Open Source Projects

Another wonderful method to work with other developers while increasing your abilities is to contribute to open source projects. You may obtain real experience and expand your CV by contributing to open source projects. Open source projects to contribute to may be found on websites such as GitHub and GitLab.

Attending Conferences and Meetups

Attending conferences and meetings in person is an excellent method to network with other frontend devs. These events provide opportunity to learn from industry professionals, participate in seminars, and network with other developers. Frontend Love and Front-Trends are two popular conferences, and you may locate meetings in your region on sites like Meetup.com.

Staying Up-to-date with the Latest Trends and Technologies

It is critical for a frontend developer to keep up with the newest trends and technology. This helps you to remain competitive while making educated judgments regarding the technologies and frameworks you employ. Here are some pointers to help you keep current:

Following Industry Leaders

Following industry experts on social media sites such as Twitter and LinkedIn is one of the greatest methods to keep updated. These industry professionals often provide information and ideas regarding the most recent developments and innovations.

Reading Relevant Blogs and Articles

Reading frontend development blogs and publications can also help you keep current. Sites such as Smashing Magazine and CSS Tricks provide useful insights and lessons on the most recent tools and methods.

Exploring New Technologies

Finally, it is critical to regularly investigate new technologies and frameworks. Trying out new tools and technologies will help you grasp their strengths and limits, as well as which tools are best suited to specific tasks.

Overcoming Common Challenges Faced by Beginners in Frontend Development

Starting a career in frontend development might be difficult, but there are strategies to overcome typical challenges. Here are some pointers for overcoming typical obstacles:

Dealing with Imposter Syndrome

New developers frequently experience imposter syndrome. To overcome it, instead of comparing yourself to others, try to focus on your own growth and successes. Keep in mind that everyone has to begin somewhere.

Managing Time and Staying Motivated

When you first start out as a frontend developer, it can be difficult to manage your time and stay motivated. Set attainable objectives for yourself and divide larger work into smaller activities to keep motivated. Consider utilizing time management software such as Trello or Asana.

Working with Legacy Code

Working with old code might be frightening, but it is a necessary skill for frontend developers to learn. Before making any modifications, attempt to grasp the purpose and structure of the code. You may also work with other developers by using technologies like GitHub's pull request functionality.

Gaining Confidence and Advancing Your Career as a Frontend Developer

Once you've gained experience as a frontend developer, it's critical to gain confidence and move your career forward. Here are some suggestions:

Building a Strong Online Presence

Creating a strong online presence might assist you in showcasing your work and connecting with possible employers. Consider developing a personal website and distributing your work via social media channels such as Twitter and LinkedIn.

Applying for Jobs and Navigating Interviews

Make sure to highlight your relevant experience and skills when applying for frontend development jobs. Prepare to share how you've dealt with specific issues in the past and display your problem-solving abilities during interviews.

Continuing Your Education and Professional Development

Finally, maintaining your education and professional growth is critical for your advancement. To learn new skills and keep current in the field, consider attending workshops, taking courses, or obtaining certifications. Sites such as Udemy and Coursera provide a diverse selection of online courses.

Conclusion

So, here's the deal: being a frontend hero takes a lot of dedication, hard work, and always being open to learning. If you follow the advice in this article, you'll be on the right track to being a successful frontend developer. To get even better, stay curious, practice regularly, and work with other developers. There are endless possibilities in frontend development, whether you're just starting out or looking to make a career change. I hope this article has given you some good ideas and motivation to chase your dreams and become a frontend hero.

FAQs

Frontend development is creating the portion of a website or web application with which users interact. This comprises the user interface's design, layout, and functionality, as well as the coding languages and technologies necessary to implement it.

Do I need a degree in computer science to become a frontend developer?

No, a computer science degree is not required to work as a frontend developer. Many successful frontend developers learned their trade through online courses, bootcamps, or self-study. However, understanding the technical aspects of frontend development can be aided by a solid foundation in computer science concepts.

What are the most important skills for a frontend developer?

Frontend developers must be proficient in HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, as well as have knowledge with major frontend frameworks and libraries. Strong problem-solving abilities, attention to detail, and excellent communication skills are also essential for every developer.

How can I stay up-to-date with the latest trends and technologies in frontend development?

Keeping up with the newest trends and technology in frontend development necessitates ongoing study and experimentation. Following industry experts on social media and reading relevant blogs and articles are two efficient ways to keep updated. Attending conferences and meetings, as well as cooperating with other developers, may also give significant insights and opportunity to learn about current advances in the area.

Thank you for reading!
Soumyadeep Mandal @imsampro

Items.Title: 指令和依赖注入的超能力：第 6 部分 | Superpowers with Directives and Dependency Injection: Part 6
Items.Description:
该是关于 Angular 指令及其近乎神奇的超能力的系列文章的第 6 部分了。
今天我们将探索一个不太流行但非常非常普遍且有效的用例场景——将业务逻辑引入指令中。

介绍

首先，让我们反思一下我们已经做了什么，并了解今天的案例有何根本不同。

在之前的文章中，我们创建了指令来完成一些特定的、可重用的、非常有用的事情。

例如，我们构建了一个指令

  • 检查密码强度
  • 当宿主元素滚动到视图中时发出自定义事件
  • 将模板视图传输到另一个组件
  • 显示一个装载机
  • 在现有组件上添加了一些默认输入

如您所见，所有这些情况都有一个共同的主题——所有这些指令都是超级可重用的，因为它们可以放在任何项目中并且可以正常工作； 它们中没有一个真正包含任何业务逻辑。 但在很多情况下，对于现实生活中的项目，我们希望可重用和易于共享的是业务逻辑。 那么有哪些场景呢？

使用指令处理权限

想象一下，我们的应用程序有权启用或禁用某些功能。 例如，我们可能有一项功能允许用户编辑他们的个人资料，但前提是他们拥有正确的权限。 我们有一些PermissionService有一个hasPermission方法，它接受一个权限名称并返回一个布尔值。 我们可以像这样在我们的组件中使用这个服务：

 @Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div *ngIf="hasPermission('edit-profile')"> <button (click)="editProfile()">Edit profile</button> </div> ` }) export class ProfileComponent { constructor(private permissionService: PermissionService) {} hasPermission(permissionName: string) { return this.permissionService.hasPermission(permissionName); } editProfile() { // ... } }

但是现在每次我们想要检查这个或那个权限时，我们都需要注入服务，也许编写一个包装方法，从模板中调用它，或者其他一些稍微麻烦的方式。 此外，如果我们访问这些权限的方式发生变化（例如，服务现在具有不同的 API 接口），我们可能会面临相当大的重构。 此外，如果我们有某种与 Observables 一起工作的状态管理解决方案（例如 NgRx），我们的代码将变得更加复杂。

那么我们如何处理呢？

好吧，如果我们可以这样做，那不是很好吗：

 @Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div *hasPermission="'edit-profile'"> <button (click)="editProfile()">Edit profile</button> </div> ` }) export class ProfileComponent { editProfile() { // ... } }

所以现在我们可以将权限名称作为输入传递给指令，指令将处理其余部分。 让我们看看如何做到这一点。

  • 首先，我们需要创建一个注入PermissionService的指令，并具有一个接受权限名称的输入属性
  • 接下来，我们将添加ngIf作为 HostDirective，以便它可以处理显示/隐藏模板
  • 最后，我们将注入对NgIf指令的引用，并将结果布尔值传递给它

我们开工吧：

 @Directive({ selector: '[hasPermission]', standalone: true, hostDirectives: [NgIf], }) export class HasPermissionDirective { private readonly permissionService = inject(PermissionService); private readonly ngIfRef = inject(NgIf); @Input() set hasPermission(permissionName: string) { // we can use any other approach here this.ngIfRef.ngIf = this.permissionService.hasPermission( permissionName, ); } }

现在，我们的指令几乎可以完美运行。 我们需要添加什么？ 当然，也可以处理用户没有权限的情况。 我们可以通过在我们的指令中添加一个else模板来做到这一点：

 @Directive({ selector: '[hasPermission]', standalone: true, hostDirectives: [NgIf], }) export class HasPermissionDirective { private readonly permissionService = inject(PermissionService); private readonly ngIfRef = inject(NgIf); @Input() set hasPermission(permissionName: string) { this.ngIfRef.ngIf = this.permissionService.hasPermission( permissionName, ); } // the improtant part @Input() set hasPermissionElse(template: TemplateRef<any>) { this.ngIfRef.ngIfElse = template; } }

现在，我们基本上只是执行业务逻辑并将结果传递给NgIf指令。 这是一个带有预览的工作示例：

使用指令处理组件中的共享数据

想象一下，我们使用一些 UI 库来显示下拉菜单：

 @Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div> <third-party-dropdown [items]="dropdownItems"></third-party-dropdown> </div> ` }) export class ProfileComponent { dropdownItems = [ { label: 'Item 1', value: 1 }, { label: 'Item 2', value: 2 } ]; }

在某些情况下，我们需要将相同的选项列表传递给多个下拉列表。 例如，我们在本文前面遇到的相同权限列表在很多地方可能非常有用：

 @Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div> <third-party-dropdown [items]="permissions"></third-party-dropdown> </div> <div> ` }) export class SomeComponent { permissionService = inject(PermissionService); permissions = this.permissionService.getPermissions(); }

然后我们真的可以在整个应用程序的多个地方看到相同的代码。 那么我们怎样才能使它更具可重用性呢？ 好吧，我们可以创建一个组件来包装下拉列表并接受项目列表作为输入：

 <app-wrapper-around-third-party-dropdown [items]="permissions"></app-wrapper-around-third-party-dropdown>

但这会带来一系列问题：

  • 最终必须向上和向下传递所有第三方下拉菜单的输入和输出
  • 我们的模板更加复杂

那么我们可以做什么呢？ 好吧，我们可以创建一个指令，将项目列表注入第三方下拉列表：

 @Directive({ selector: 'third-party-dropdown[permissionList]', standalone: true, }) export class PermissionsDropdownDirective implements OnInit { thirdPartyDropdown = inject(ThirdPartyDropdown); permissionService = inject(PermissionService); ngOnInit() { this.thirdPartyDropdown.items = this.permissionService.getPermissions(); } }

现在我们可以像这样使用它：

 <third-party-dropdown permissionList></third-party-dropdown>

就是这样！ 现在我们的模板很简单， third-party-dropdown仍然和以前一样，我们还为它增加了可重用的功能。

这是带有预览的示例：

结论

我们已经探索了指令给我们的大量可能性。 虽然还有很多，但对于不同规模的项目来说这些已经足够了，所以本系列文章即将结束。 在下一篇和最后一篇中，我们将详细讨论指令选择器，我们可以使用什么，我们如何组合不同的选择器，以及有哪些陷阱或我们不能使用某个选择器的地方。 敬请关注！



原文:

Original cover photo by Ivan Bandura on Unsplash.

It's time for the 6th installment of this article series about Angular directives - and their almost magical superpowers.
Today we are going to explore a less popular, but very, very common and valid use case scenario - bringing business logic into the directives.

Introduction

First of all, let's reflect on what we have already done, and understand how today's cases are fundamentally different.

In previous articles, we created directives that did some specific, reusable, and very useful things.

For example, we built a directive that

  • checked for password strength
  • emitted custom events when the host element scrolled into view
  • transported a template view into another component
  • displayed a loader
  • added some default inputs onto existing components

As you can see, there is a common theme for all these cases - all those directives are super-reusable in the sense that they can be dropped in any project and just work; non of them really contain any business logic. But in a lot of cases, with real-life projects, it is the business logic that we want to be reusable and easily shareable. So what scenarios are there?

Using directives to handle permissions

Imagine that our app has permissions to enable or disable certain features. For example, we might have a feature that allows users to edit their profile, but only if they have the right permissions. We have a few PermissionService that has a hasPermission method that takes a permission name and returns a boolean. We could use this service in our component like this:

@Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div *ngIf="hasPermission('edit-profile')"> <button (click)="editProfile()">Edit profile</button> </div> `
})
export class ProfileComponent { constructor(private permissionService: PermissionService) {} hasPermission(permissionName: string) { return this.permissionService.hasPermission(permissionName); } editProfile() { // ... }
}

But now every time we want to check for this or that permission, we need to inject the service, maybe write a wrapper method, call it from the template, or some other mildly cumbersome way. Also, if the way how we access those permissions changes (for example, the service now has a different API interface), we might face a pretty large refactoring. Also, if were have some sort of state management solution that works with Observables (like NgRx, for instance), our code will become even more complex.

So how do we deal with this?

Well, wouldn't it be nice if we could just do this:

@Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div *hasPermission="'edit-profile'"> <button (click)="editProfile()">Edit profile</button> </div> `
})
export class ProfileComponent { editProfile() { // ... }
}

So now we can pass the permission name as an input to the directive, and the directive will handle the rest. Let's see how we can do this.

  • First, we need to create a directive that injects the PermissionService and has an input property that will accept the permission name
  • next, we will add ngIf as a HostDirective so it can handle showing/hiding the template
  • and finally, we will inject the reference to the NgIf directive, and pass the resulting boolean to it

Let's do this:

@Directive({ selector: '[hasPermission]', standalone: true, hostDirectives: [NgIf],
})
export class HasPermissionDirective { private readonly permissionService = inject(PermissionService); private readonly ngIfRef = inject(NgIf); @Input() set hasPermission(permissionName: string) { // we can use any other approach here this.ngIfRef.ngIf = this.permissionService.hasPermission( permissionName, ); }
}

Now, our directive works almost perfectly. What do we need to add? Of course, a possibility to handle the case when the user does not have the permission. We can do this by adding an else template to our directive:

@Directive({ selector: '[hasPermission]', standalone: true, hostDirectives: [NgIf],
})
export class HasPermissionDirective { private readonly permissionService = inject(PermissionService); private readonly ngIfRef = inject(NgIf); @Input() set hasPermission(permissionName: string) { this.ngIfRef.ngIf = this.permissionService.hasPermission( permissionName, ); } // the improtant part @Input() set hasPermissionElse(template: TemplateRef<any>) { this.ngIfRef.ngIfElse = template; }
}

Now, we essentially just do the business logic and pass the result to the NgIf directive. Here is a working example with a preview:

Using directives to handle shared data in components

Imagine we use some UI library that allows us to show dropdowns:

@Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div> <third-party-dropdown [items]="dropdownItems"></third-party-dropdown> </div> `
})
export class ProfileComponent { dropdownItems = [ { label: 'Item 1', value: 1 }, { label: 'Item 2', value: 2 } ]; }

And in some cases, we need to pass the same list of options to multiple dropdowns. For example, the same list of permissions we encountered earlier in this article could be really useful in lots of places:

@Component({ selector: 'app-profile', template: ` <div> <third-party-dropdown [items]="permissions"></third-party-dropdown> </div> <div> `
})
export class SomeComponent { permissionService = inject(PermissionService); permissions = this.permissionService.getPermissions();
}

And then we could really see this same code in multiple places throughout our app. So how can we make this more reusable? Well, we could create a component that will wrap the dropdown and accept the list of items as an input:

<app-wrapper-around-third-party-dropdown [items]="permissions"></app-wrapper-around-third-party-dropdown>

But that would introduce a set of problems:

  • CSS encapsulation
  • Having to pass eventually all of the third-party dropdown's inputs and outputs up and down
  • More complexity in our templates

So what can we do instead? Well, we can create a directive that will inject the list of items into the third-party dropdown:

@Directive({ selector: 'third-party-dropdown[permissionList]', standalone: true,
})
export class PermissionsDropdownDirective implements OnInit { thirdPartyDropdown = inject(ThirdPartyDropdown); permissionService = inject(PermissionService); ngOnInit() { this.thirdPartyDropdown.items = this.permissionService.getPermissions(); }
}

And now we can use it like this:

<third-party-dropdown permissionList></third-party-dropdown>

And that's it! Now our template is simple, the third-party-dropdown is still as it used to be, and we got additional reusable functionality for it.

Here is the example with a preview:

Conclusion

We have explored a huge amount of possibilities that directives give us. While there are plenty more, this much is fairly enough for projects of different sizes, so this article series is nearing its end. In the next and last one, we will talk in detail about directive selectors, what we can use, how can we combine different selectors, and what are some pitfalls or where we cannot use a certain selector. Stay tuned!

Items.Title: 如何使用 prefers-reduced-motion 使您的网站更易于访问 | How to Make Your Website More Accessible with prefers-reduced-motion
Items.Description:
普尼特索尼

发表于 5 月 2 日

如果您正在构建网站或 Web 应用程序，请务必确保每个人都可以使用它们，无论他们可能面临任何可访问性挑战。 可能影响某些用户的一个可访问性问题是由页面上的动画和其他运动效果引起的晕动病或不适。 这就是prefers-reduced-motion用武之地。

理解prefers-reduced-motion

如果用户请求减少运动，则应禁用或缩小页面上的任何动画或其他运动效果。

下面是如何在 CSS 中使用prefers-reduced-motion的示例：

 @media (prefers-reduced-motion) { /* Disable or scale back animations and motion effects */ }

以下是如何使用prefers-reduced-motion来创建更易于访问的用户体验的一些示例：

  • 如果您的页面上有幻灯片或轮播，您可以在prefers-reduced-motion设置为 reduce 时禁用自动动画，这样用户就可以手动播放幻灯片而不会被自动动画效果轰炸。

  • 如果你有一个滑入和滑出视图的导航菜单，你可以在prefers-reduced-motion设置为 reduce 时禁用滑动动画，这样菜单立即出现而没有任何运动效果。

  • 如果您的链接或按钮有悬停效果，您可以禁用该效果或在prefers-reduced-motion设置为 reduce 时将其更改为更微妙的效果，这样用户就不会体验到任何突然或分散注意力的动作。

更喜欢减少运动演示

如果启用了操作系统的动画，您可以看到平滑的滚动动画。
如果禁用操作系统的动画，平滑滚动将不再可见

结论：

通过在您的 CSS 中使用prefers-reduced-motion ，您可以使您的网站或 Web 应用程序更具包容性，并且对于可能因动画和其他运动效果而感到不适或晕车的用户而言更易于访问。 它是一个简单但功能强大的工具，可以帮助您打造更受欢迎、更具包容性的在线体验。

原文:

Punit Soni

Posted on May 2

   

If you're building websites or web applications, it's important to make sure that everyone can use them, regardless of any accessibility challenges they may face. One accessibility issue that can affect some users is motion sickness or discomfort caused by animations and other motion effects on the page. That's where prefers-reduced-motion comes in.

Understanding prefers-reduced-motion

prefers-reduced-motion is a media query that can be used in CSS to detect whether the user has requested reduced motion on their device or browser. The possible values for prefers-reduced-motion are no-preference and reduce. If the user has requested reduced motion, any animations or other motion effects on the page should be disabled or scaled back.

Here's an example of how to use prefers-reduced-motion in CSS:

@media (prefers-reduced-motion) { /* Disable or scale back animations and motion effects */
}

Examples of prefers-reduced-motion in action

Here are some examples of how you can use prefers-reduced-motion to create a more accessible user experience:

  • If you have a slideshow or carousel on your page, you can disable automatic animation when prefers-reduced-motion is set to reduce, so that the user can manually advance the slides without being bombarded with automatic motion effects.

  • If you have a navigation menu that slides in and out of view, you can disable the sliding animation when prefers-reduced-motion is set to reduce, so that the menu appears instantly without any motion effects.

  • If you have a hover effect on your links or buttons, you can disable the effect or change it to a more subtle effect when prefers-reduced-motion is set to reduce, so that the user doesn't experience any sudden or distracting motion.

prefers-reduced-motion demo

You can see the smooth scroll animation if you operating system's Animation is enabled.
If you disable the operating system's Animation, smooth scrolling will no longer visible

Enable Disable Animation in Windows

Conclusion:

By using prefers-reduced-motion in your CSS, you can make your website or web application more inclusive and accessible to users who may experience discomfort or motion sickness from animations and other motion effects. It's a simple but powerful tool that can help you create a more welcoming and inclusive online experience.

Items.Description:
穆罕默德阿纳斯
